store_fields: Mongo corpus_readiness.status = 'fully_enriched' (per-corpus rollup, collection corpus_readiness, schema corpus_readiness.v2)
rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a): one string, three scopes — document ingest_stage value, corpus readiness status value, and a batch-state boolean. Grep hits are not interchangeable. Plan S6 explicitly wants fully_enriched to STOP implying librarian-grade metadata; today it already means only operational completeness (artifacts+projections+verification), never bibliographic quality.
rows.evidence: readiness.py:368-454 shows fully_enriched is purely operational (counts min-gated by lexicon_ready, summary_tree_index_ready, document_profile_index_ready); no bibliographic/card field participates.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (b) HIGH RISK: no strict_ready symbol exists anywhere in backend (grep -rniE 'strict[_ -]read' backend/ = zero hits), yet ~80% of its definition is ALREADY computed by compute_corpus_readiness (queryable/summary/tree-sync/lexicon-projection/no-failed-jobs/denominator exclusions, readiness.py:1105+). Building strict_ready as a parallel computation would duplicate corpus_readiness.v2. It should be an extension of that snapshot (add: ontology-violation count, extraction/concept promotion gate — 'unpromoted_extraction_docs' already counted at readiness.py:456-457,1008 — and 3-tier smoke-probe receipts). Failure mode (a) inside the plan itself: 'strict_ready' is ALSO the top value of the temporal capability enum (temporal_unavailable|partial|strict_ready, checklist:210) — same word, corpus publication gate vs temporal capability tier.
rows.evidence: Checklist:1252-1268 lists 10 gates; readiness.py already measures 8 of them; missing: ontology predicate/entity-type violations, tier smoke/negative-control probes.

rows.canonical: corpus_readiness (for the enrichment/operational meaning); qualify all others (retrieval_readiness, inference_ready, extraction deploy-readiness, lexicon_ready)
rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a) CONFIRMED, 5-way: 'readiness' names five distinct mechanisms. Worst sub-collision: retrieval_readiness.py's class is literally named CorpusReadinessReport (:29) while ingestion/readiness.py owns the 'corpus_readiness' Mongo collection — same noun, disjoint semantics (schema-exists vs enrichment-complete). Any new plan work saying 'readiness' must pick a qualifier.
rows.evidence: Five files, five meanings, all grep 'readiness': readiness.py:1 ('Computed corpus-readiness contract'), retrieval_readiness.py:1 ('Retrieval readiness guards'), health_service.py:239 (P1.8 tri-state), extraction_validation.py:1 (deploy-readiness probes), corpus_lexicon.py:2966 ('publish readiness' = flip lexicon_state).

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (b): 'operational readiness' has NO code symbol of its own — it is a plan-side rename of the existing corpus_readiness.v2 computation. S6's readiness split should be implemented as a field split INSIDE corpus_readiness (e.g. readiness.operational / readiness.metadata_quality / readiness.temporal), not as a new service; otherwise the same gates get computed twice (readiness.py already reconciles projections via lexicon_ready, summary_tree_index_ready, document_profile_index_ready mins at :376,:441,:454).
rows.evidence: PLAN_CRITIQUE S6 defines operational readiness as 'artifacts/projections reconcile' — that is verbatim what readiness.py measures today; it just publishes the result under the name fully_enriched.

Nearest seams: (1) librarian_cards presence per doc (card_builder.py), (2) the S2/T-HOOK-3 bibliographic backfill fields once they land on documents, (3) facet-precision leftovers from P0.5 decontamination. store_fields: Mongo ghost_b_extractions.temporal_captures + temporal_capture_version (both write sites _persist_extraction_rows and mongo_writer.stash_ghost_b, per ghost_b.py:2084-2087 comment)
The capability enum store (temporal_unavailable|partial|strict_ready) DOES NOT EXIST
rows.collision_note: Two collisions. (a) 'temporal_support' already exists in models/schemas.py meaning 'this graph packet's evidence has dates' — do not reuse that name for the capability tier. (a-bis) the enum's top value 'strict_ready' collides with the corpus-level Definition Of Strict Ready (see strict ready row): one word, two gates at two scopes. Recommend the enum live as corpus_readiness.temporal_capability with values temporal_unavailable|temporal_partial|temporal_strict to break the collision.
rows.evidence: grep for temporal_unavailable|temporal_class|temporal_scope|source_published_at|strict_ready in backend/ = zero hits; only capture-side fields (T-HOOK-1) exist.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a): the verb 'publish' already has a DIFFERENT code meaning in this family — corpus_lexicon.finalize_corpus_lexicon_index docstring 'reconcile ... then publish readiness' (corpus_lexicon.py:2966) means flipping lexicon_state to lexicon_ready, not card publication. A 'publication gate' grep will hit the lexicon path first. To-be-built: a check in build_librarian_cards/card consumption that requires corpus_readiness.status (or future strict_ready/metadata_quality legs) before cards become retrieval-visible.

One member is PLAN-ONLY: 'scheduled ownership reconciliation with report-only default' (checklist:515, unchecked) — nearest seam scripts/orphan_ownership_manifest.py (P0.6 dry-run manifest) + services/ingestion/repair_scheduler.py.
